nl: Pre-recorded content is a copyright violation.
All Nobel physics laureates are full-time scientists.
TOra is under the GNU General Public License.
Everything that requires talent requires practice.
Tower A is neither a building in New Haven nor a skyscraper in Manhattan.
All wage earners are human.
All humans are capable of abstract thoughts.
A neuroimaging technique is either an invasive neuroimaging technique or a noninvasive neuroimaging technique.
All sci-fi movies are movies.
Rhos Aelwyd F.C. is a Welsh football club.
All certified public accountants have good business sense.
Everyone at the business conference is either an investor or an entrepreneur.
Modus Ponens is a component of a major part of reasoning rule.
siv_canonical_fol: exists x.(exists y.((MajorPartOf(x, y) & ReasoningRule(y))) & ComponentOf(modusPonens, x))

nl: Tissues are soft.
Everyone who took the bar exam is knowledgeable about criminal procedures.
All books written by Neil Gaiman have sold more than one thousand copies.
gold_fol_normalized: all x ((Book(x) & WrittenBy(x, neilGaiman)) -> exists y (MoreThan(y, num1000) & SoldCopies(x, y)))

nl: If a European plays football, they play what Americans call soccer.
siv_canonical_fol: all x.(European(x) -> (exists y.(Football(y) & Plays(x, y)) -> exists z.(Soccer(z) & Plays(x, z))))
gold_fol_normalized: all x (FootballPlayer(x) & European(x) -> exists y (Call(american, y, soccer) & Play(x, y)))

nl: All monitors in the library are made before 2010.
gold_fol_normalized: all x ((Monitor(x) & In(x, library)) -> ProducedBefore(x, yr2010))

nl: If Robin's friends study hard, then they grew up with parents who worked as doctors.
gold_fol_normalized: all x (RobinsFriends(x) & StudyHard(x) -> exists y exists z (-(y=z) & GrowUpWith(x, y) & GrowUpWith(x, z) & ParentOf(y, x) & ParentOf(z, x) & Doctor(y) & Doctor(z)))

nl: All of this brand's products produced in the US are sold in the US.
siv_canonical_fol: all x.((ProductOfBrand(x) & ProducedIn(x, us)) -> SoldIn(x, us))
gold_fol_normalized: all x ((ThisBrand(x) & Product(x) & ProducedIn(x, us)) -> SoldIn(x, us))

nl: None of the easy Leetcode problems have an AC rate lower than 20 percent.
Top soccer players are soccer players who can use both the left foot and right foot very efficiently.
siv_canonical_fol: all x.(TopSoccerPlayer(x) -> (SoccerPlayer(x) & exists y.(LeftFoot(y) & CanUseEfficiently(x, y)) & exists z.(RightFoot(z) & CanUseEfficiently(x, z))))
gold_fol_normalized: all x (SoccerPlayer(x) & UseEfficiently(x, leftFoot) & UseEfficiently(x, rightFoot) -> TopSoccerPlayer(x))

nl: People born and living in New York City are New Yorkers.
siv_canonical_fol: all x.((Person(x) & BornIn(x, newYorkCity) & LiveIn(x, newYorkCity)) -> NewYorker(x))
gold_fol_normalized: all x ((BornIn(x, newYorkCity) & LiveIn(x, newYorkCity)) -> NewYorker(x))

nl: If two soccer teams score the same number of goals in one UCL final during both regular and extra time, they need to play the penalty shoot-out.
gold_fol_normalized: all x all y (SoccerTeam(x) & SoccerTeam(y) & SameScore(x, y) & During(regularTime) & During(extraTime) -> PlayPenalty(x, y))

nl: If a horse falls in a race, it poses risks to its rider.
siv_canonical_fol: all x.(Horse(x) -> exists y.((Race(y) & FallsIn(x, y)) & exists z.(RiderOf(z, x) & PosesRisksTo(x, z))))
gold_fol_normalized: all x (Horse(x) & InRace(x) & Falls(x) -> PoseRiskTo(x, rider))

nl: If the meal is popular at the party, then it is delicious.
gold_fol_normalized: all x all y (Meal(y) & PopularAt(y, party) -> Delicious(y))

nl: Some fruits sold in New Haven are shipped from Mexico.
siv_canonical_fol: exists x.((Fruit(x) & SoldIn(x, newHaven)) & ShippedFrom(x, mexico))
gold_fol_normalized: exists x exists y (Fruit(x) & SoldIn(x, newHaven) & ShippedFrom(x, mexico) & (-(x=y)) & Fruit(y) & SoldIn(y, newHaven) & ShippedFrom(y, mexico))

nl: Flightless birds cannot fly over a vast distance.
If someone in Love City is good with pets, then they are not scared of animals.
gold_fol_normalized: all x ((GoodWith(x, pet) & In(x, loveCity)) -> -ScaredOf(x, animal))

nl: If someone has been scratched or bitten by some entity, they have been attacked by that entity.
gold_fol_normalized: all x exists y (BittenBy(x, y) | ScratchedBy(x, y)) -> AttackedBy(x,y)

nl: All customers in James' family who subscribe to AMC A-List are eligible to watch three movies every week without any additional fees.
siv_canonical_fol: all x.((Customer(x) & InFamily(x, james) & SubscribeTo(x, amcAList)) -> all y.(Movie(y) -> EligibleToWatch(x, y)))
gold_fol_normalized: all x ((Customer(x) & In(x, jameSFamily) & SubscribedTo(x, aMCAList)) -> EligibleForThreeFreeMoviesEveryWeekWithoutAdditionalFees(x))

nl: Every terrifying building on Halloween is a creepy haunted house.
A werewolf is a human that can turn into a wolf.
siv_canonical_fol: all x.(Werewolf(x) -> exists y.((Human(x) & Wolf(y)) & CanTurnInto(x, y)))

nl: All working people who hate working for others want to be entrepreneurs.
gold_fol_normalized: all x (exists y exists z (-(y=x) & -(z=x) & -(y=z) & HateWorkingFor(x, y) & HateWorkingFor(x, z)) -> Entrepreneur(x))

nl: If you go somewhere by car and meet a traffic jam, you will lose time.
Maya would only play the violin if her fingers could never be injured.
Atlanta is in Georgia.
If John is not a PhD student, then he is not a member of the university.
Ryan is performing at New Haven Symphony Orchestra.
Daniel studied bioengineering during his undergraduate at Rice University.
Los Angeles is a city in California.
Fort Carillon was located in New France.
Mongolia was where Ambiortus Dementjevi lived.
Hugh Vanstone is from the UK.
Jack is entitled to the right to life and liberty, has human rights, or knows about the first-in-first-out data structure.

Lithium is either a non-rare earth element and essential for exploring future directions of electronics, or is not a non-rare earth element and is not essential for exploring future directions of electronics.
Peter Parker is either a superhero or a civilian.
James is a customer who is not between the ages of 60 and 80.
gold_fol_normalized: Customer(james) & (-exists y(Between(y, num60, num80) & Age(james, y)))

nl: All people who attend weddings are getting married or know the people who are getting married.
siv_canonical_fol: all x.(exists y.((Person(x) & Attend(x, y) & Wedding(y))) -> (GettingMarried(x) | exists z.(GettingMarried(z) & Know(x, z))))
gold_fol_normalized: all x (Attend(x, wedding) -> GettingMarried(x) | (exists y (Know(x, y) & GettingMarried(y)))

nl: All professional centerbacks are professional soccer defenders.
